title: Foods Toxic to Dogs — Safety Reference
note: This reference is not exhaustive. When in doubt, consult your veterinarian. In an emergency, call the ASPCA Animal Poison Control Center at 1-888-426-4435.

rows.symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, seizures, rapid heart rate, death
rows.action: Emergency vet immediately

rows.symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, restlessness, increased urination
rows.action: Contact vet promptly

rows.symptoms: Vomiting, lethargy, acute kidney failure
rows.action: Emergency vet immediately — even one grape can be fatal

rows.symptoms: Vomiting, lethargy, acute kidney failure
rows.action: Emergency vet immediately

rows.symptoms: Rapid insulin release, low blood sugar, seizures, liver failure
rows.action: Emergency vet immediately — acts within 30 minutes

rows.symptoms: Hemolytic anemia, weakness, pale gums, collapse
rows.action: Contact vet — toxic cooked or raw, all forms

rows.symptoms: Hemolytic anemia, GI upset, weakness, pale gums
rows.action: Contact vet — 5× more toxic than onions per weight

rows.symptoms: Anemia, GI upset, weakness
rows.action: Contact vet

rows.symptoms: Weakness, hyperthermia, vomiting, tremors, inability to walk
rows.action: Contact vet — symptoms within 12 hours

rows.symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, fluid accumulation; pit is a choking hazard
rows.action: Monitor; contact vet if symptomatic

rows.symptoms: Vomiting, disorientation, breathing difficulty, coma, death
rows.action: Emergency vet immediately

rows.symptoms: Rapid heart rate, seizures, tremors, death
rows.action: Emergency vet immediately

rows.symptoms: Bloat, GI rupture risk, alcohol toxicity
rows.action: Emergency vet — dough continues rising in stomach

rows.symptoms: GI perforation, choking, internal injury
rows.action: Monitor closely; vet if any distress — never give cooked bones

rows.symptoms: Cyanide poisoning — dilated pupils, panting, shock
rows.action: Remove seeds; contact vet if seeds were consumed

rows.symptoms: Dilated pupils, difficulty breathing, shock, death
rows.action: Emergency vet if pits ingested

rows.symptoms: Cyanide poisoning, respiratory distress
rows.action: Emergency vet if pit ingested; also choking risk

rows.symptoms: Excessive thirst, vomiting, diarrhea, seizures (large amounts)
rows.action: Contact vet if large amount consumed; ensure water access

rows.symptoms: Hallucinations, disorientation, increased heart rate, seizures
rows.action: Contact vet

rows.symptoms: Malignant hyperthermia, panting, seizures, death (especially Greyhounds)
rows.action: Emergency vet immediately

rows.symptoms: Liver failure, kidney failure, death
rows.action: Emergency vet immediately — collect mushroom sample for ID

rows.symptoms: Fever, vomiting, diarrhea, swollen lymph nodes, death if untreated
rows.action: Emergency vet — only in Pacific Northwest USA

rows.symptoms: Tremors, seizures, vomiting, high temperature
rows.action: Contact vet — other cheeses lower risk

rows.symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, depression (small amounts may only cause GI upset)
rows.action: Contact vet if large amount consumed

rows.symptoms: Tremors, seizures, vomiting
rows.action: Emergency vet if moldy walnuts consumed

rows.symptoms: GI upset, drooling, weakness, slow heart rate
rows.action: Contact vet — ripe tomato fruit is low risk

rows.symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, weakness, confusion
rows.action: Contact vet — cooked plain potato is generally safe

rows.symptoms: Drooling, vomiting, diarrhea, kidney failure, tremors
rows.action: Contact vet

rows.symptoms: GI obstruction from seeds, enteritis
rows.action: Contact vet if seeds ingested

rows.toxic_compound: Iron, other vitamins in toxic doses
rows.symptoms: Vomiting blood, diarrhea, organ damage
rows.action: Emergency vet — never give human vitamins to dogs

rows.symptoms: Vomiting, tremors, seizures, respiratory failure, death
rows.action: Emergency vet immediately — e-cig liquids especially concentrated

rows.symptoms: Disorientation, dilated pupils, urine dribbling, vomiting, coma
rows.action: Contact vet — rarely fatal but distressing; edibles more dangerous

rows.symptoms: Rapid hypoglycemia, seizures, liver failure
rows.action: Emergency vet immediately — check ingredient list

rows.symptoms: GI blockage — vomiting, straining, abdominal pain
rows.action: Emergency vet if large piece swallowed — corn kernels are safe

rows.symptoms: GI upset, diarrhea — sugar-free versions may contain xylitol (Critical)
rows.action: Small amounts low risk; always check for xylitol in sugar-free

rows.symptoms: Tremors, seizures, vomiting, hyperthermia
rows.action: Contact vet — the 'garbage toxicosis' — secure trash cans
